description: The bird first was noticed when it made a deep chip call similar to the call of a Dark-eyed Junco. I looked for the supposed Junco and found a small warbler. Brownish-olive on the back and crown. Darker brownish-black on the wings and tail. There was a distinct small white patch at the base of the primaries. The underparts were a buffy tan color transitioning to a lighter whitish tan in the vent area. There was a noticeable whitish buff supercilium over the eye. The throat was a lighter buff than the rest of the underparts. The bill was thin, pointed and black like typical warbler bills. It foraged throughout the Russian Olive thicket from 2 feet to 8 feet off the ground.
